# Mandatory Evidence Checklist for Replacement Sign-Off

**Bead**: `asupersync-2oh2u.1.2.5` ([T1.2.c])
**Generated by**: SapphireHill (claude-code / opus-4.6)
**Date**: 2026-03-03
**Dependencies**: T1.2.a (functional contracts), T1.2.b (non-functional criteria)
**Purpose**: Specify the exact evidence set required before marking any capability
gap as closed. Includes failure policy for missing evidence.
**T1.2 synthesis**: consolidated domain sign-off matrix is tracked in
`docs/tokio_functional_parity_contract.md` section "Domain Definition-of-Done
Synthesis (T1.2 Parent)".

---

## 1. Evidence Categories

Every capability gap closure requires evidence from ALL applicable categories.
Missing evidence blocks sign-off unless an explicit waiver is recorded.

| Code | Category | Required For |
|------|----------|-------------|
| E1 | Functional conformance tests | All domains |
| E2 | Cancellation correctness tests | All async domains |
| E3 | Deterministic replay traces | All domains with lab support |
| E4 | Integration/E2E tests | All domains |
| E5 | Benchmark results with delta | Domains with NF criteria |
| E6 | Documentation update | All domains |
| E7 | Migration guide | All domains |
| E8 | API stability review | All public API changes |

---

## 2. Per-Category Requirements

### E1 — Functional Conformance Tests

| Req | Detail |
|-----|--------|
| E1.1 | At least one test per MUST requirement in the functional contract (C01-C28) |
| E1.2 | Tests are in `conformance/src/tests/` or `tests/` (not just inline `#[cfg(test)]`) |
| E1.3 | Tests run in CI without flakes for 10 consecutive runs |
| E1.4 | Test names reference contract IDs (e.g., `test_c03_1_bounded_mpsc_send_blocks`) |
| Artifact | Test suite file path + CI run ID showing green |

### E2 — Cancellation Correctness Tests

| Req | Detail |
|-----|--------|
| E2.1 | Verify that cancel during operation produces `Outcome::Cancelled` (not data loss) |
| E2.2 | Verify cleanup budget is respected |
| E2.3 | Verify two-phase operations (reserve/commit) are cancel-safe |
| E2.4 | Lab oracle suite passes: `obligation_leak_oracle`, `quiescence_oracle` |
| Artifact | LabRuntime test output with oracle results |

### E3 — Deterministic Replay Traces

| Req | Detail |
|-----|--------|
| E3.1 | At least one LabRuntime test with fixed seed produces deterministic trace |
| E3.2 | Replaying the trace with same seed yields identical execution |
| E3.3 | Trace includes: task spawn/complete events, cancel events, timer fires |
| Artifact | `.trace` file in `target/traces/` + replay verification output |

### E4 — Integration / E2E Tests

| Req | Detail |
|-----|--------|
| E4.1 | At least one E2E test that exercises the capability end-to-end |
| E4.2 | E2E test covers happy path + at least one error/failure path |
| E4.3 | Test uses real I/O (not mocks) for networking/database/messaging domains |
| Artifact | Test file path + CI run ID |

### E5 — Benchmark Results

| Req | Detail |
|-----|--------|
| E5.1 | Criterion benchmark exists for each NF metric with hard ceiling |
| E5.2 | Benchmark runs on standardized hardware (documented spec) |
| E5.3 | Results show all hard ceilings met |
| E5.4 | Delta vs. baseline is recorded (first run establishes baseline) |
| Artifact | `target/criterion/` output + comparison report |

### E6 — Documentation Update

| Req | Detail |
|-----|--------|
| E6.1 | README coverage map row reflects current parity status |
| E6.2 | Module-level doc comments describe API and usage |
| E6.3 | Any Asupersync-specific behavior differences from Tokio are documented |
| Artifact | Git diff showing doc changes |

### E7 — Migration Guide

| Req | Detail |
|-----|--------|
| E7.1 | Step-by-step instructions for migrating from Tokio equivalent |
| E7.2 | Code examples showing before (Tokio) and after (Asupersync) |
| E7.3 | Known limitations and workarounds documented |
| Artifact | Section in `docs/migration/` or inline docs |

### E8 — API Stability Review

| Req | Detail |
|-----|--------|
| E8.1 | Public API surface is reviewed for naming consistency |
| E8.2 | Breaking changes are flagged and versioned |
| E8.3 | Deprecated APIs have migration path documented |
| Artifact | Review comment in bead or PR |

---

## 3. Domain Applicability Matrix

Not all categories apply to every domain. This matrix shows which evidence
is mandatory (M), recommended (R), or not applicable (N/A).

| Domain | E1 | E2 | E3 | E4 | E5 | E6 | E7 | E8 |
|--------|----|----|----|----|----|----|----|----|
| F01 Runtime | M | M | M | M | M | M | M | M |
| F02 Cancellation | M | M | M | M | R | M | N/A | M |
| F03 Channels | M | M | M | M | M | M | M | M |
| F04 Sync | M | M | M | M | M | M | M | M |
| F05 Time | M | M | M | M | M | M | M | M |
| F06 I/O | M | M | R | M | M | M | M | M |
| F07 Codec | M | M | R | M | R | M | M | M |
| F08 Bytes | M | N/A | N/A | R | M | M | M | M |
| F09 Reactor | M | R | M | M | M | M | R | M |
| F10 Net | M | M | R | M | M | M | M | M |
| F11 DNS | M | R | R | M | R | M | M | M |
| F12 TLS | M | M | R | M | M | M | M | M |
| F13 WebSocket | M | M | R | M | R | M | M | M |
| F14 HTTP | M | M | R | M | M | M | M | M |
| F15 QUIC/H3 | M | M | M | M | M | M | M | M |
| F16 Web | M | M | R | M | M | M | M | M |
| F17 gRPC | M | M | R | M | M | M | M | M |
| F18 Database | M | M | R | M | M | M | M | M |
| F19 Messaging | M | M | R | M | R | M | M | M |
| F20 Service | M | M | M | M | M | M | M | M |
| F21 Filesystem | M | M | R | M | R | M | M | M |
| F22 Process | M | R | R | M | R | M | M | M |
| F23 Signals | M | R | R | M | R | M | M | M |
| F24 Streams | M | M | M | M | R | M | M | M |
| F25 Observability | M | R | R | M | R | M | M | M |
| F26 Lab | M | M | M | M | R | M | N/A | M |
| F27 Combinators | M | M | M | M | M | M | M | M |
| F28 Interop | M | M | R | M | M | M | M | M |

---

## 4. Sign-Off Protocol

### 4.1 Individual Gap Closure

1. Implementer completes work and produces evidence artifacts
2. Implementer creates a sign-off bead comment listing all evidence by category
3. Evidence is verified (tests pass in CI, benchmarks meet thresholds)
4. Reviewer (different agent) confirms evidence completeness
5. Gap bead is closed with sign-off record

### 4.2 Evidence Format

```markdown
## Sign-Off Evidence for [Gap ID]

### E1 — Conformance Tests
- File: `tests/capability_conformance.rs`
- CI run: [link or run ID]
- Tests: N passing, 0 failing

### E2 — Cancellation Tests
- File: `tests/capability_cancel.rs`
- Oracle: obligation_leak=OK, quiescence=OK

### E3 — Replay Traces
- Trace: `target/traces/capability_seed42.trace`
- Replay match: 100% (10 runs)

[... remaining categories ...]
```

### 4.3 Waiver Policy

If a mandatory evidence item cannot be produced:

1. Document the specific item and reason it cannot be produced
2. Assess risk impact (does absence increase migration risk?)
3. Record compensating control (alternative evidence or planned follow-up)
4. Obtain explicit approval from project owner
5. Track waiver in gap bead with `waiver` label

### 4.4 Failure Policy

| Condition | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Any E1 test fails | Gap remains open; fix and re-verify |
| E2 oracle fails | Gap remains open; cancellation bug |
| E3 replay diverges | Gap remains open; non-determinism bug |
| E5 benchmark exceeds hard ceiling | Gap remains open; performance regression |
| E6/E7 missing | Gap may close with `docs-pending` label; 7-day SLA |
| E8 missing | Gap may close with `review-pending` label; 3-day SLA |

---

## 5. Aggregate Closure Gate

The full Tokio-replacement claim requires:

1. All 28 capability families at R1 or R2 risk level (no R3/R4)
2. All MUST functional contracts verified (E1)
3. All hard ceiling benchmarks met (E5)
4. Zero open waivers for E1, E2, or E3 categories
5. Migration guides published for all domains with >100 crates.io dependents
6. Final replacement claim RFC reviewed and signed (T9.8)
